title: RBAC v2: structured denial reason codes + optional authz debug header
preview: Problem: With RBAC v2 deny-by-default landing across Gateway + Control Plane, Support and Console UX need consistent, structured signals to answer “why was I denied?” without leaking policy internals or creating side-channel disclosure. Today we return a generic 403 from several stacks and each service/gateway path ...
text: Problem: With RBAC v2 deny-by-default landing across Gateway + Control Plane, Support and Console UX need consistent, structured signals to answer “why was I denied?” without leaking policy internals or creating side-channel disclosure. Today we return a generic 403 from several stacks and each service/gateway path formats errors differently, making troubleshooting slow (and leading to tickets like SUP-48305/INT-19022). This PR introduces a shared set of denial reason codes and a gated, optional debug header mechanism for admin troubleshooting. Summary of changes: (1) Add canonical AuthzDenialReasonCode enum (and mapping helpers) in the shared authz package. (2) Standardize PermissionDenied error payload shape across gateway + control-plane HTTP handlers (no policy details; includes stable reason code + request id). (3) Add opt-in debug response headers behind BOTH a feature flag and ...

title: Retention: add change history + tenant export endpoint (for compliance report pack)
preview: Context: The Compliance Reporting Pack MVP needs a deterministic, customer-ready artifact that captures (a) the tenant’s effective retention settings across supported data classes and (b) the auditability of retention changes over time. Today we can fetch the current policy, but we don’t retain a first-class change ...
text: Context: The Compliance Reporting Pack MVP needs a deterministic, customer-ready artifact that captures (a) the tenant’s effective retention settings across supported data classes and (b) the auditability of retention changes over time. Today we can fetch the current policy, but we don’t retain a first-class change history nor provide a stable export payload suitable for report generation. This PR adds: (1) retention policy change history persisted in the control plane with immutability guarantees, and (2) a new export endpoint that produces a versioned export (JSON) used by the report pack generator and audit-log-exporter attachment bundling. Scope: control plane only; does not change TTL enforcement in data plane stores. What’s included: 1) Change history: new table retention_policy_change_events with append-only rows capturing actor, source, diff summary, previous_policy_hash, new_...

title: Bootstrap audit exporter service skeleton + CI + local dev harness
preview:
## Summary
Bootstraps the new `audit-log-exporter` service repository with a minimal runnable skeleton, CI workflows, a base configuration model, and a local dev harness to support early iteration.

This is the first landing step for ENG-5139. No adapters, no delivery semantics, and no production wiring in this PR; ...
text:
## Summary
Bootstraps the new `audit-log-exporter` service repository with a minimal runnable skeleton, CI workflows, a base configuration model, and a local dev harness to support early iteration.

This is the first landing step for ENG-5139. No adapters, no delivery semantics, and no production wiring in this PR; those will follow in subsequent PRs.

## Motivation / Context
We need a dedicated service to read audit events (initially via stubbed source in local dev), apply tenant scoping, and deliver to downstream destinations with retries/checkpointing. Before we can iterate on architecture and adapters, we need:
- Standard project layout + build tooling
- Baseline CI (lint/test/build, container build)
- A config model (typed) that can evolve with control-plane config sync
- A local harness so devs can run the service end-to-end without needing the full control plane

## What’s incl...

title: Add audit log events for residency policy create/update/delete + wire to audit-log-exporter
preview: Context: As part of Hosted data residency enforcement we need auditable change history for residency pinning configuration (org/project/api-key level). This PR emits audit log events on residency policy mutations (create/update/delete) and wires those events into the existing audit-log-exporter pipeline so they are ...
text: Context: As part of Hosted data residency enforcement we need auditable change history for residency pinning configuration (org/project/api-key level). This PR emits audit log events on residency policy mutations (create/update/delete) and wires those events into the existing audit-log-exporter pipeline so they are available in Console audit log views and customer exports. Scope: Control plane (residency policy service) emits events; exporter normalizes/ships; no changes to request-time enforcement here. Event types (v1): residency_policy.created, residency_policy.updated, residency_policy.deleted, residency_policy.override_applied (admin-only, gated behind flag). Payload highlights: actor (user_id/service, auth_type, ip where available), target (org_id, project_id nullable, api_key_id nullable), policy_id, allowed_regions, primary_region, allow_cross_region_fallback, effective_from, ...

title: Retention policy evaluator + cache (precedence, invalidation, unit tests)
preview: Summary: Adds a retention policy evaluation module to the control plane with an in-memory cache + explicit invalidation. Includes unit tests that lock down precedence/override rules and edge cases (region overrides, disabled logging modes, legacy TTL fields). This is the shared library used by policy CRUD endpoints,...
text:
Summary: Adds a retention policy evaluation module to the control plane with an in-memory cache + explicit invalidation. Includes unit tests that lock down precedence/override rules and edge cases (region overrides, disabled logging modes, legacy TTL fields). This is the shared library used by policy CRUD endpoints, TTL enforcement config resolution, and export/report surfaces.

Motivation: We need a single authoritative place to evaluate effective retention settings per tenant + deployment mode + region. The evaluator also needs to be fast enough for the request path (config lookups and downstream enforcement jobs) without pushing policy logic into each data store.

Key behavior (precedence rules): (1) Explicit tenant policy (most specific scope wins: data_class+region > data_class > global) (2) Tenant logging config TTL fields (legacy) ONLY when no explicit retention policy exists f...

title: Add TTL/retention policy references to tenant logging config model
preview:
Title: Add TTL/retention policy references to tenant logging config model

Context
Retention controls requires a single place to express what data classes are eligible for storage and (when stored) what retention policy applies. Today our per-tenant logging config describes enablement + sampling/fields for request l...
text:
Title: Add TTL/retention policy references to tenant logging config model

Context
Retention controls requires a single place to express what data classes are eligible for storage and (when stored) what retention policy applies. Today our per-tenant logging config describes enablement + sampling/fields for request logs / traces / etc, but it has no concept of retention TTL or a link to the retention policy service.

This PR extends the tenant logging config model and API surfaces to include optional TTL/retention references. It also includes a safe, backwards-compatible migration path so older SDKs/clients keep working.

Scope
- Adds fields to TenantLoggingConfig to reference a retention policy and/or per-store TTL overrides.
- Adds storage + migrations (DB columns + JSON blob) and read/write plumbing.
- Adds compatibility behavior so the API continues accepting legacy payloads.
- Add...

title: Add retention policy CRUD endpoints + validation + audit trail
preview: Summary: This PR adds the first cut of retention policy management to the control plane: CRUD endpoints, request/response types, validation rules, and audit log events for all policy mutations. This unblocks wiring retention enforcement in downstream stores and enables the compliance reporting pack to export tenant ...
text:
Summary: This PR adds the first cut of retention policy management to the control plane: CRUD endpoints, request/response types, validation rules, and audit log events for all policy mutations. This unblocks wiring retention enforcement in downstream stores and enables the compliance reporting pack to export tenant retention configuration.

Context: As part of the Retention controls + compliance reporting pack project (ENG-18420 / PM-842), we need a durable, auditable policy object that can be referenced by logging/telemetry subsystems. Policy evaluation and caching will be layered in a follow-up PR (see pr-28427) but the API surface and auditability need to land first so dependent teams can integrate.

What’s included: (1) RetentionPolicy model + persistence in control-plane DB (tenant-scoped, immutable id, mutable spec). (2) REST endpoints: POST /v1/tenants/{tenant_id}/retention-pol...

title: Add Datadog Logs destination adapter + audit tag/facet mapping
preview: Summary: This PR adds a Datadog Logs destination adapter to the audit-log-exporter and establishes a stable mapping from audit event schema v2 -> Datadog tags/facets. It also introduces rate-limit aware batching behavior (primarily for 429/503) so we degrade gracefully under Datadog intake constraints while preservi...
text: Summary: This PR adds a Datadog Logs destination adapter to the audit-log-exporter and establishes a stable mapping from audit event schema v2 -> Datadog tags/facets. It also introduces rate-limit aware batching behavior (primarily for 429/503) so we degrade gracefully under Datadog intake constraints while preserving at-least-once semantics. Context: As part of the SIEM-ready audit export beta, we support Splunk HEC, S3, and Datadog Logs. Datadog differs from Splunk in that (a) intake is strongly rate-limited, and (b) the best user experience is via consistent tagging/faceting for filtering/dashboards in Datadog. This PR aligns with ENG-5156 and the delivery semantics ADR (at-least-once, downstream dedup via event_id). Design notes (high-level): Adapter: Adds `datadog_logs` destination type with `site`, `intake_path`, and `api_key_secret_ref`. We send batches to `https://http-intake....

title: Routing: enforce residency region pinning + disable cross-region fallback by default
preview: Summary: Integrates Hosted Data Residency “region pinning” policy evaluation into Smart Routing decisions. Requests are constrained to the policy’s allowed_regions and will no longer fail over cross-region by default. Adds structured decision context to routing logs/spans for traceability and downstream error shapin...
text:
Summary: Integrates Hosted Data Residency “region pinning” policy evaluation into Smart Routing decisions. Requests are constrained to the policy’s allowed_regions and will no longer fail over cross-region by default. Adds structured decision context to routing logs/spans for traceability and downstream error shaping.

Why: We’ve had customer incidents where routing attempted cross-region fallback when a customer expected EU-only processing (see SUP-28441). This PR ensures routing respects residency constraints deterministically, and makes the decision observable (selected region, policy source, and block reason).

Behavior changes: 1) Policy evaluation: Router calls ResidencyPolicyService (cached) at request admission to resolve the effective residency policy (API key > project > org precedence). 2) Region selection: Candidate regions list is intersected with policy.allowed_regions. ...

title: Hotfix: close authz bypass on streaming chat route (deny-by-default + regression test)
preview: Incident: See Slack #incidents thread “authz regression on streaming endpoint” (sources/slack/incidents/1740151207-authz-regression-on-streaming-endpoint.json). During RBAC v2 rollout, we discovered that one streaming code path could start an SSE response without having a permission check applied. This was not an in...
text:
Incident: See Slack #incidents thread “authz regression on streaming endpoint” (sources/slack/incidents/1740151207-authz-regression-on-streaming-endpoint.json). During RBAC v2 rollout, we discovered that one streaming code path could start an SSE response without having a permission check applied. This was not an intentional allowlist; it was a routing/middleware gap that effectively bypassed deny-by-default for a specific handler. Impact: unauthorized callers could receive streamed tokens on the affected route if they had valid authentication but lacked the required permission; audit logs still captured request metadata but did not record an authz decision for these responses because evaluation never ran.

Root cause: The streaming handler for chat completions registers under a separate router group (legacy streaming mux) which did not have the gateway authz middleware attached. Non-...

title: Filtering engine v1: allowlist/denylist by category/action/resource
preview: Implements filtering engine v1 for SIEM-ready audit export (ENG-5160). This adds a small, explicit rule model (allowlist/denylist) that can be evaluated per-event in the exporter with deterministic semantics and safe defaults. Scope in this PR: (1) filter model + evaluation logic (categories/actions/resource predica...
text: Implements filtering engine v1 for SIEM-ready audit export (ENG-5160). This adds a small, explicit rule model (allowlist/denylist) that can be evaluated per-event in the exporter with deterministic semantics and safe defaults. Scope in this PR: (1) filter model + evaluation logic (categories/actions/resource predicates), (2) strict config validation + normalization (including “deny wins” and invalid rule rejection), and (3) comprehensive unit tests for tricky cases (empty filters, conflicting allow/deny, unknown categories, wildcard handling, resource/action normalization, timestamp boundary behavior). Motivation: we need customer-configurable filtering that is auditable, easy to reason about during incident response, and stable enough to map to UI + control-plane API without frequent behavior changes. Semantics (as implemented): The effective policy is evaluated in this order: valida...

title: Add Splunk HEC destination adapter (gzip compression, 429 backoff, per-token circuit breaker)
preview: Summary: Implements a new Splunk HEC destination adapter for SIEM-ready audit exports. The adapter supports gzip-compressed payloads, robust handling of downstream throttling (429) with Retry-After-aware exponential backoff + jitter, and a per-token circuit breaker to avoid hot-looping on bad credentials or persiste...
text: Summary: Implements a new Splunk HEC destination adapter for SIEM-ready audit exports. The adapter supports gzip-compressed payloads, robust handling of downstream throttling (429) with Retry-After-aware exponential backoff + jitter, and a per-token circuit breaker to avoid hot-looping on bad credentials or persistent throttling. Motivation: Beta customers are primarily Splunk-first and we observed exporter lag spikes when HEC returns sustained 429s; this change improves delivery stability and reduces noisy retry behavior. Scope: Adapter implementation + config schema + basic unit tests + metrics hooks. Not included: Splunk indexer acknowledgment (HEC ACK) support (will track separately), advanced event field remapping beyond the audit schema’s SIEM mapping guidance. Delivery semantics: remains at-least-once; downstream Splunk consumers should dedupe on event_id if needed. Config: add...

title: Add dead-letter queue + guarded replay CLI for failed export batches
preview: Summary: This PR introduces a dead-letter queue (DLQ) for audit export batches that fail delivery after exhausting retries, and adds an operator-facing replay tool with guardrails. This is a reliability/safety feature for the SIEM-ready audit export beta (Splunk HEC / Datadog Logs / S3) to ensure we never silently d...
text: Summary: This PR introduces a dead-letter queue (DLQ) for audit export batches that fail delivery after exhausting retries, and adds an operator-facing replay tool with guardrails. This is a reliability/safety feature for the SIEM-ready audit export beta (Splunk HEC / Datadog Logs / S3) to ensure we never silently drop events while still protecting the platform and customers from accidental replay storms. Context / Motivation: During downstream outages (e.g., sustained 429s from Splunk HEC or 403s from mis-scoped Datadog keys), the exporter currently retries with backoff and then surfaces a per-destination error. Without DLQ persistence we risk either (a) losing batches on process/queue churn or (b) having no safe operational path to replay specific failures once a customer fixes credentials/quotas. ENG-5164 calls for a DLQ + replay tooling with explicit operator controls. Design high...

title: Support backup-oriented batch export mode (pagination + retention guardrails)
preview: Context: Private DR / compliance asks require a repeatable, non-interactive way to export audit logs for backup/retention evidence. The existing exporter is optimized for ad-hoc, interactive exports and can be slow/fragile when used in scheduled jobs (offset-based pagination, no checkpointing, limited safeguards aro...
text:
Context: Private DR / compliance asks require a repeatable, non-interactive way to export audit logs for backup/retention evidence. The existing exporter is optimized for ad-hoc, interactive exports and can be slow/fragile when used in scheduled jobs (offset-based pagination, no checkpointing, limited safeguards around retention boundaries).

This PR adds a backup-oriented batch export mode designed for scheduled backups:
- New CLI subcommand: `audit-log-exporter export batch`
- Cursor-based pagination using the API’s stable `after_id`/`next_cursor` semantics (no offset)
- Checkpointing/resume: writes a checkpoint token to disk periodically; supports `--resume` to continue without duplicating output
- Retention guardrails: validates requested time window against server-reported retention (if available) and fails fast with actionable messaging; explicit override flag required to procee...

title: Emit metrics + tracing for Private backup/restore operations (with error categorization)
preview: Motivation: SRE/on-call and customers need a consistent way to answer “are backups running?” and “why did the last restore drill fail?” across VPC + on-prem Private installs. We already log backup/restore steps in the installer, but logs alone are hard to alert on and don’t support reliable aggregation. This PR adds...
text: Motivation: SRE/on-call and customers need a consistent way to answer “are backups running?” and “why did the last restore drill fail?” across VPC + on-prem Private installs. We already log backup/restore steps in the installer, but logs alone are hard to alert on and don’t support reliable aggregation. This PR adds: (1) structured Prometheus metrics for backup + restore operations (success/failure, duration, bytes, last success timestamps), (2) OpenTelemetry tracing spans with consistent attributes for per-step visibility, and (3) explicit error categorization to drive dashboards/alerts and reduce pager noise. Scope: This instruments the control-plane backup/restore path (installer-driven + control-plane endpoints) and adds common helpers used by both “backup create” and “restore apply”. It does not change backup artifact format or encryption behavior. Metrics (Prometheus): - redwood...

title: Private: expose backup status endpoints from control plane
preview: Motivation: Private customers (and our own SRE/Solutions workflows) need a consistent way to surface “backup readiness” without scraping CronJob logs or inferring state from storage backends. This PR adds control-plane API endpoints that report backup status and last-success timestamps per tenant/installation, with ...
text:
Motivation: Private customers (and our own SRE/Solutions workflows) need a consistent way to surface “backup readiness” without scraping CronJob logs or inferring state from storage backends. This PR adds control-plane API endpoints that report backup status and last-success timestamps per tenant/installation, with strict authz and safe redaction (no bucket names, object keys, or secret material). This unblocks Console UI + automated restore drills to gate on “recent successful backup” and to page on backup job regressions.

What’s included: Adds a BackupStatus read model persisted by the backup orchestrator after each run (success/failure), keyed by tenant_id and environment_id; Read-only endpoints under the Private API surface; Includes timestamps (last_success_at, last_attempt_at), status enum, last_error_category + last_error_message (truncated), artifact metadata (format_version,...

title: gateway: add RBAC v2 authz middleware (deny-by-default) + standardized 403s
control-plane: add residency policy schema + validation + admin endpoints (with migrations)
preview: Summary: Introduces first-class residency policies in the control plane, including schema + persistence, validation rules, admin CRUD endpoints, and a migration path for existing orgs/projects. This is the foundational layer used by routing/gateway enforcement (separate PRs) and by Console settings UX. Key points: (...
text: Summary: Introduces first-class residency policies in the control plane, including schema + persistence, validation rules, admin CRUD endpoints, and a migration path for existing orgs/projects. This is the foundational layer used by routing/gateway enforcement (separate PRs) and by Console settings UX. Key points: (1) New entity: ResidencyPolicy with scope=org|project|api_key, allowed_regions[] + primary_region, and explicit fallback controls. (2) Validation: deterministic, fail-closed on invalid configs; prevents primary_region not in allowed set; blocks ambiguous overlaps at same scope; enforces precedence rules (api_key > project > org) while allowing inherit semantics. (3) Admin API: internal-only endpoints behind RBAC permission control_plane.residency_policy:read|write; supports list/get/create/update/delete and dry-run validate. (4) Migration: seeds default “inherit/unrestricte...

title: RBAC v2: add permission enum + registry (metadata + CI validation)
preview: Summary: Introduces a canonical permission registry for RBAC v2 in the redwood monorepo. This adds a typed Permission enum (backed by stable string IDs), a registry with required metadata (description, owning team, sensitivity tier, allowed scopes), and CI validation to enforce uniqueness + naming conventions. Motiv...
text: Summary: Introduces a canonical permission registry for RBAC v2 in the redwood monorepo. This adds a typed Permission enum (backed by stable string IDs), a registry with required metadata (description, owning team, sensitivity tier, allowed scopes), and CI validation to enforce uniqueness + naming conventions. Motivation: We need a single source of truth for "what permissions exist" before we can (a) annotate endpoints/jobs consistently, (b) generate role templates/policy bundles, and (c) provide audit/evidence artifacts for enterprise customers. This PR intentionally includes only an initial catalog covering the first critical workflows (org/user/role management, API key management, audit log export, routing policy edits) so downstream adoption work can proceed in parallel. Design notes: Permission IDs follow ADR-014 conventions (namespaced dot-separated strings, no implicit wildcard...

title: Add differential prioritizer + regression demux CI hook for stratified smoke sampling
preview: Context and motivation: We continue to see noisy regression alerts from broad smoke-suites where rare but high-variance prompts swamp triage cycles. This PR introduces a sampling prioritizer that differentially weights prompts by historical variance, recent degradation signal, and business impact metadata, and a CI ...
text:
Context and motivation: We continue to see noisy regression alerts from broad smoke-suites where rare but high-variance prompts swamp triage cycles. This PR introduces a sampling prioritizer that differentially weights prompts by historical variance, recent degradation signal, and business impact metadata, and a CI hook (regression-demux) that demultiplexes failing prompts into per-model/cohort lanes for targeted replays and quarantines. The goal is to reduce alert fatigue while preserving early detection for high-impact regressions.

What this PR does (high level): implements eval_harness.samplers.differential_prioritizer, extends sample metadata to carry priority scores and buckets, adds a ci/hooks/regression_demux adapter that: (1) inspects CI eval failures, (2) enriches failures with priority and cohort context, (3) routes items to dedicated replay queues and labeling workflows, a...
